lines.en_lit: GEMARA: taught: to teach him taking handfuls Rav Pappa said: The High Priest had two chambers. One the Parhedrin chamber and one the Chamber of the House of Avtinas. One in the north and one in the south. One the north, as we learned There were six chambers in the courtyard, three in the north and three in the south.

in the south the Chamber of the Salt, the Chamber of the Parva, the Chamber of the Rinsers. The Chamber of the Salt where would place the salt for the offering The Chamber of the Parva, where they salted the consecrated hides And on the roof was the Hall of Immersion for the High Priest for Yom Kippur. The Chamber of the Rinsers where they would rinse the innards of consecrated From there a circular staircase ascended to the roof of the Chamber of the Parva.

three in the north the Chamber of the Wood, the Chamber of the Exile, the Chamber of Hewn Stone. the Chamber of the Wood, Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov, said: I forgot what would serve. Abba Shaul says: The High Priest’s chamber was behind the two and the roofs of three of them were level.

the Chamber of the Exile there was the well exile, and the wheel was positioned over it. And from there, supply water for in the entire courtyard the Chamber of Hewn Stone, there Sanhedrin of Israel convened and judged the priests in whom a disqualification was detected would wear black and would wrap himself in black and would emerge and go his way, And in whom a disqualification was not found would wear white and wrap himself in white and would enter and serve with his fellow priests.

one in the south as we learned Seven gates were in the courtyard, three in the north and three in the south and one in the east. in the south the Gate of Fire; second to it the Gate of the Offering; third to it the Gate of the Water. that was in the east Gate of Nicanor, and two chambers were there one to its right and one to its left. One the Chamber of Pineḥas the Dresser, and one the Chamber of the Preparers of the Griddle-Cake Offering,

that were in the north: the Gate of the Spark, a portico building, And a second story built atop it, and there the priests guarding from above Levites from below. Inside of the rampart, Second to the Gate of the Offering; third to it was the Gate of the Hall of the Hearth.

And it was taught The High Priest would five immersions and ten sanctifications on that day, And each of these in the sacred on the roof of the Hall of the Parva, except for this which was in the non-sacred on the roof of the Gate of the Water, was alongside his chamber.

but we do not know whether the Parhedrin chamber was in the north and the Chamber of the House of Avtinas was in the south, or the Chamber of the House of Avtinas was in the north and the Parhedrin chamber was in the south. And, it is reasonable the Parhedrin chamber was in the south

What is the reason arises early and he covers his legs, and immerses And walks to the north and learns the taking handfuls. And he comes to the Temple and performs the service for the entire day. Toward the evening they sprinkle upon him, and he then walks to the south and immerses and rests

As, if you say Chamber of Parhedrin in the north, he arises early, and he covers his legs. he walks to the south and immerses. And he learns taking handfuls. And he comes to the Temple and performs the service for the entire day. Toward the evening they sprinkle upon him, and he then walks to the south and immerses, and he needs to walk back to the north and rest. Do we impose him to that extent?
Why not we impose upon him more, that if he is a Sadducee, he will retire.
Or, alternatively, that he will not become haughty As, if you do not say let us establish both of next to one another; or, alternatively, one sufficient for him.

said to him: My Master, High Priest. Let us say that this is a conclusive refutation of Rav Huna, son of Rav Yehoshua, as Rav Huna, son of Rav Yehoshua, said: These priests are the agents of the Merciful One. As, if you say they are our agents,
